blockers.title: Guardian confirmation links are hard-coded to production
blockers.evidence: `convex/guardians.ts:216` builds the emailed confirm URL from `SITE`; `site.config.mjs:4` fixes `SITE` to `https://www.waiorg.me`. The confirm UI uses the frontend's `PUBLIC_CONVEX_URL` (`src/portal/convex.ts:5-6`), so a staging/dev token can send the guardian to the live domain or wrong backend and fail confirmation. This breaks AC2 and the required staged dry run before production.
blockers.required_fix: Use a deployment-specific public web origin for transactional guardian links, e.g. the same configured `SITE_URL` used for auth, fail closed if absent, and add a test proving the emailed token link targets the current frontend/backend pair.

blockers.title: Failed resend attempts bypass the per-member throttle and can burn the shared send cap
blockers.evidence: AC4 requires max 1/hour and 3/day per member. On Resend failure, `rollbackGuardianSend` releases both member buckets (`convex/guardians.ts:246-249`), and the test codifies immediate retry success (`tests/convex/guardianConsent.test.ts:346-359`). Each failed prepare has already consumed the shared `signin24h:global` bucket (`convex/guardians.ts:152-177`), and rollback does not release it, so one pending account can loop provider failures until magic-link and guardian sends are globally denied without any delivered email.
blockers.required_fix: Keep token rollback, but enforce a bounded per-member failure-attempt throttle and make global-budget semantics match delivered sends. Add tests for repeated Resend failures and shared-cap preservation.

non_blocking_notes: `npm test -- --run tests/unit/guardianToken.test.ts tests/convex/guardianConsent.test.ts` and `npm run build` could not run in this read-only sandbox because Vite/Astro attempted to write generated files.
No committed secret values found in the targeted scan; the only em dash found in the diff is inside a test assertion checking that email copy has none.

spec_compliance.criterion: AC1 guardian email at the right moment with vault copy
spec_compliance.evidence: `beforeSessionCreation` schedules `internal.guardians.sendGuardianEmail` when a minor reaches `pending_guardian`; guardian email subject/body match the vault draft.
The button mutation is present and lookup is neutral, but the emailed token link is hard-coded to `https://www.waiorg.me`, which can target the wrong deployment/backend.

spec_compliance.criterion: AC3 confirmation activates member, certificate, audit
spec_compliance.evidence: `confirmGuardianConsent` patches consent proof and member age/lifecycle fields, writes audit rows, and issues the certificate in one mutation.
Resend failures release the per-member throttle while still consuming the shared global cap, allowing repeated failed attempts to deny later sends.
Active minor members get the AFG signpost plus certificate section and bypass adult tiles/settings.
Vault-drafted guardian and youth strings are materially present; no member-facing em dashes found.
No at-18 graduation, event/photo reconfirmation, revocation portal, or admin queue was added.
Tests cover core token paths, but they codify immediate retry after Resend failure and do not cover deployment-origin correctness for emailed confirmation links.

summary: Do not ship. The core consent mutation is close, but this minors gate can break staged confirmation links and has a resend failure path that can bypass the member throttle while exhausting the shared send budget.
